metadata.paper_title: A Web-based Reference Architecture for Mobile Learning: Its Quality Aspects and Evaluation
metadata.venue: 2017 IEEE International Conference on Software Architecture Workshops (ICSAW)

system_context.system_name: Web-based Reference Architecture for Mobile Learning
system_context.system_description: A web-based reference architecture implementing the workflow of preparation, activity, analysis & reflection for mobile data collection activities (e.g., field trips).
system_context.business_goals: To provide a common foundation for mobile learning applications, addressing challenges of device fragmentation (BYOD), dynamic requirements, portability, flexibility, and expandability.

evaluation_setup.evaluation_goal: To analyze and verify that the requirements (portability, adaptability, reusability, extensibility) are met in the design and to identify suitable evaluation methods.

architectures.name: Reference Architecture for Mobile Learning
architectures.description: Core components (Preparation, Activity, Analysis & Reflection) operate independently as frontend solutions and communicate via a central Interface (RESTful API) with the Backend (Server, Data Persistence, Services).

key_components.responsibilities: Frontend component for visualizing and analyzing data.
Communication between core components and backend (recommended RESTful API).
Data persistence, server logic, authentication, and other services.

assumptions: Web technologies are recommended for core components.
RESTful API is recommended for the interface.

scenarios.scenario_text: Run all frontend components (preparation, activity, and analysis & reflection) using web technologies exclusively to support multiple devices (mLearn4web/PELARS).
Visualize collected data on a SMART board.
Use a native Android application for the activity component instead of a web application (TriGO).
Reuse mLearn4web components for PELARS, adding a new matrix module to the preparation component.
Add a new reflection component developed by visualization experts (PELARS).

scenario_classification.rationale: Explicitly supported by the architecture's recommendation of web technologies, satisfying portability.
Supported by the web-based nature of the architecture which runs on emerging technologies like SMART boards.
Supported by the Interface component which can provide/consume data to/from any device or native application.
Required minor modifications (adding a module) to the component, showing extensibility/reusability.
Architecture supports the addition/exchange of components via the Interface without changing the architectural structure.

scenario_evaluations.change_description: Added a new module (matrix) to the authoring tool; added an additional layer of activity organization.
A new reflection component was developed and added.

architectural_strengths: High degree of portability due to web technology focus.
Adaptability to new devices (e.g., SMART boards) and native apps.
High reusability of components (e.g., mLearn4web to PELARS).
High extensibility allowing new modules and component exchange.

recommendations: Use web technologies in all core components.
Use a RESTful API as an interface.

inference_notes: Inferred 'Indirect' classification for S-04 because the paper mentions 'modifications were implemented', implying work was done on the component.
Inferred 'Direct' for S-03 and S-05 as the architecture accommodated the different/new components via the defined Interface without structural change.
The paper uses 'candidate architectures' to refer to the implementations (scenarios) used to build/validate the Reference Architecture.
